story: That afternoon, while Heather drove to town, I found a brittle note in Uncle George’s telescope case: “If Milly ever hears me on a February night, don’t bar the woods. I’ll come by the cellar and take my old girl to see the Dog Star again.” I put it back. That night, fresh snow covered the yard, and a single line of bootprints came out of the woods and stopped at the locked cellar hatch. Milly pawed at them until we dragged her inside. I warmed my hands over the embers and told Heather what I’d seen.
implausible_knowledge: a brittle note in Uncle George’s telescope case: “If Milly ever hears me on a February night, don’t bar the woods. I’ll come by the cellar and take my old girl to see the Dog Star again.”
question: What will Heather most plausibly do next to figure out what caused the tracks?
answer_with_knowledge: Open the door for Milly and head outside toward the woods, treating the tracks as George coming for her.
answer_without_knowledge: Suggest an ordinary explanation and go check the basement and cellar hatch for signs of entry.
